017package org.apache.juneau.marshall;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
020
021import java.util.*;
022
023/**
024 * Supported wire formats for {@link Enum} values.
025 *
026 * <p>
027 * Used by {@link MarshallingContext.Builder#enumFormat(EnumFormat)},
028 * {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.Marshalled#enumFormat()},
029 * {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshalledProp#enumFormat()}, and
030 * {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshalledConfig#enumFormat()} to control how
031 * {@link Enum} values are written to and read from the wire.
032 *
033 * <p>
034 * The default is {@link #TO_STRING} which preserves the historical wire output produced by
035 * {@code Object.toString()} (the previous default before this enum existed). New APIs SHOULD prefer
036 * {@link #NAME} for stability against {@code toString()} overrides.
037 *
038 * <p>
039 * Numeric formats ({@link #ORDINAL}) emit native numeric wire types on binary serializers
040 * (BSON / CBOR / MsgPack) and a bare integer literal on text formats. All other constants emit a
041 * UTF-8 string.
042 *
043 * <p>
044 * Parsers SHALL accept any wire shape that the corresponding serializer can produce, regardless of
045 * the parser-side {@code EnumFormat} setting. The parser-side setting is consulted only for genuinely
046 * ambiguous input.
047 */
048public enum EnumFormat {
049
050   /** Sentinel meaning "no value configured" — falls through to the next-higher precedence level. */
051   NOT_SET,
052
053   /** Calls {@link Object#toString()} on the enum value (the default). */
054   TO_STRING,
055
056   /** Calls {@link Enum#name()} on the enum value. */
057   NAME,
058
059   /** Lower-case hyphenated form: {@code MY_ENUM_VALUE} → {@code "my-enum-value"}. */
060   LOWER_HYPHEN,
061
062   /** Upper-case hyphenated form: {@code MY_ENUM_VALUE} → {@code "MY-ENUM-VALUE"}. */
063   UPPER_HYPHEN,
064
065   /** Lower-case underscored form: {@code MY_ENUM_VALUE} → {@code "my_enum_value"}. */
066   LOWER_UNDERSCORE,
067
068   /** Lower-case form using {@link Locale#ROOT}: {@code MY_ENUM_VALUE} → {@code "my_enum_value"}. */
069   LOWER,
070
071   /** Upper-case form using {@link Locale#ROOT}: equivalent to {@link Enum#name()} for canonical UPPER_SNAKE enum names. */
072   UPPER,
073
074   /** {@link Enum#ordinal()} as a numeric value. */
075   ORDINAL;
076
077   /**
078    * Formats the specified enum value using this format.
079    *
080    * @param value The enum value.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
081    * @return The formatted wire representation, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code value} is <jk>null</jk>.
082    */
083   public String format(Enum<?> value) {
084      if (value == null)
085         return null;
086      return switch (this) {
087         case NOT_SET, TO_STRING -> value.toString();
088         case NAME, UPPER -> value.name();
089         case LOWER_HYPHEN -> value.name().toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT).replace('_', '-');
090         case UPPER_HYPHEN -> value.name().replace('_', '-');
091         case LOWER_UNDERSCORE, LOWER -> value.name().toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT);
092         case ORDINAL -> Integer.toString(value.ordinal());
093      };
094   }
095
096   /**
097    * Parses the specified wire value into an enum constant of the specified class.
098    *
099    * <p>
100    * Parsing is format-agnostic: every constant accepts any of the wire shapes produced by
101    * {@link #format(Enum)} regardless of the configured constant.  Lookup order:
102    * <ol>
103    *    <li>Numeric input → resolved as {@link #ORDINAL}.
104    *    <li>Exact match against {@link Enum#name()}.
105    *    <li>Exact match against {@link Object#toString()}.
106    *    <li>Reconstruct UPPER_SNAKE form (replace {@code -} with {@code _}, upper-case) and re-try {@code name()}.
107    *    <li>Case-insensitive match against {@code name()} and {@code toString()}.
108    * </ol>
109    *
110    * @param <E> The enum class type.
111    * @param value The wire value.  Can be <jk>null</jk> or blank.
112    * @param enumClass The enum class.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
113    * @return The parsed enum constant, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code value} is <jk>null</jk> or blank.
114    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the value does not match any enum constant.
115    */
116   @SuppressWarnings({
117      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for enum format parsing dispatch
118   })
119   public static <E extends Enum<E>> E parse(String value, Class<E> enumClass) {
120      if (value == null)
121         return null;
122      var s = value.trim();
123      if (s.isEmpty())
124         return null;
125      // Numeric input: try ORDINAL first.
126      if (isAllDigits(s)) {
127         try {
128            var ord = Integer.parseInt(s);
129            var consts = enumClass.getEnumConstants();
130            if (ord >= 0 && ord < consts.length)
131               return consts[ord];
132         } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") NumberFormatException e) {
133            // Fall through to string-based matching.
134         }
135      }
136      // Exact name match.
137      for (var e : enumClass.getEnumConstants())
138         if (e.name().equals(s))
139            return e;
140      // Exact toString() match.
141      for (var e : enumClass.getEnumConstants())
142         if (s.equals(e.toString()))
143            return e;
144      // Reconstruct UPPER_SNAKE form (handles LOWER_HYPHEN / UPPER_HYPHEN / LOWER / LOWER_UNDERSCORE).
145      var snake = s.replace('-', '_').toUpperCase(Locale.ROOT);
146      for (var e : enumClass.getEnumConstants())
147         if (e.name().equals(snake))
148            return e;
149      // Case-insensitive fallback.
150      for (var e : enumClass.getEnumConstants())
151         if (e.name().equalsIgnoreCase(s) || s.equalsIgnoreCase(e.toString()))
152            return e;
153      throw illegalArg("Could not resolve enum value ''{0}'' on class ''{1}''", value, enumClass.getName());
154   }
155
156   /**
157    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this format is numeric on the wire.
158    *
159    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this format emits a numeric wire value.
160    */
161   public boolean isNumeric() {
162      return this == ORDINAL;
163   }
164
165   private static boolean isAllDigits(String s) {
166      if (s.isEmpty())
167         return false;
168      var i = s.charAt(0) == '-' || s.charAt(0) == '+' ? 1 : 0;
169      if (i == s.length())
170         return false;
171      for (; i < s.length(); i++) {
172         var c = s.charAt(i);
173         if (c < '0' || c > '9')
174            return false;
175      }
176      return true;
177   }
178}
